item.title: Energy Storage Is the Latest ‘Not in My Backyard’ Battleground
item.description: The pushback threatens to slow a major battery boom in the US.

item.title: Apple, Amazon Push Back on Stricter Emissions Reporting Rules
item.description: The tech giants and dozens more companies are resisting changes proposed by the Greenhouse Gas Protocol, a global standard-setter.

item.title: Cost to Build a Natural Gas-Fired Power Plant Surges 66%, BNEF Says
item.description: Costs to build natural gas-fired power plants in the US increased 66% between 2023 and 2025 as developers propose building more projects nationwide to meet rising electricity demand.

item.title: Climate Negotiators Seize on Fossil Fuel Crisis Ahead of COP31
item.description: Soaring oil and gas prices are helping to bolster the argument that the world needs to accelerate its move to clean energy sources.

item.title: Toxic Rain Falls on Russia’s Tuapse City in Oil Facility Blaze
item.description: Polluted rain fell on Russia’s Black Sea city of Tuapse as authorities struggle to extinguish a blaze at local oil facilities following a drone attack, highlighting the environmental effects from the war in Ukraine.

item.title: California Is Ground Zero for the Growing Battery Backlash
item.description: Locals worried about fire risks and industrialization are fighting developers looking to build hundreds of energy storage installations.

item.title: China’s March Solar Panel Additions Slow to Hit Four‑Year Low
item.description: China’s new solar installations slowed sharply to hit a four-year low for the month of March, underscoring mounting pressure across the struggling sector.

item.title: In Dakar, a Cultural Center Grows Around a Baobab Tree
item.description: Francis Kéré’s design for the Goethe-Institut Dakar draws on the Burkinabé architect’s practice of using sustainable and climate-resilient local materials.

item.title: UK Finance Unit Sets Up $1.5 Billion Fund for Asia Energy Supply
item.description: British International Investment, the UK’s development finance institution, has created a £1.1 billion ($1.5 billion) fund to back energy transition sectors in India and Southeast Asia, with a view to partnering with pension funds and other institutional investors.

item.title: Vietnam Capital Scales Back Gas Bike Ban on Infrastructure Gaps
item.description: Vietnam’s capital is dialing back plans for a sweeping ban on gasoline-powered motorbikes as gaps in charging networks and public transport force a shift to more limited, time-based restrictions.

item.title: Lessons From One of the World’s Greenest Cities
item.description: Sadiq Khan shares how he fought for environmental policies through a tumultuous decade as London’s mayor.

item.title: China Battery-Maker Gotion Says War Shifts Focus to Clean Energy
item.description: Gotion High-Tech Co. Ltd., a major Chinese battery manufacturer, is seeing a renewed global focus on the green transition as fossil fuel disruptions due to the Iran war drive demand for clean-energy technology.

item.title: Race for Next UN Chief Heats Up With First Round of Interviews
item.description: The race to become the next leader of the United Nations moved into a higher gear with the four declared candidates facing hours of questions over two days. Top among them: can the UN be made effective again?

item.title: How Microsoft Spooked the Global Carbon Removal Market
item.description: In the early days of April, a group of mid-level employees at Microsoft Corp. started making calls that would send tremors through the global market for carbon removals.

item.title: What’s Happening With Tesla’s Battery Business
item.description: Plus, CATL’s sodium ion batteries will be rolling out on roads across China later this year.

item.title: Turkey Says COP31 Climate Summit to Focus on Clean Energy Shift
item.description: Turkey will use its role as host of this year’s COP31 climate summit to boost clean energy adoption, according to the event’s incoming president, with the Iran war showing how important it is to diversify away from fossil fuels.

item.title: Tesla’s Quiet Moneymaker, Its Battery Business, Suddenly Stalls
item.description: The company’s energy storage sales saw rapid growth, until an unexpected slowdown this year.

item.title: HSBC Climate Data Triggers Investor Call for Regulatory Review
item.description: A group of investors managing $400 billion is calling for HSBC Holdings Plc to be subjected to a regulatory review, citing concerns the UK bank may be misleading shareholders and creditors about its exposure to near-term climate risks.

item.title: EU to Make Carbon Market ‘Fit for Future,’ Climate Chief Says
item.description: The European Union will use an upcoming reform of its carbon market to ensure that the extra support for heavy-emitting industries is repaid through a commitment to investing in the region’s green transition.

item.title: Coffee Giants Will Map World’s Farms to Fight Deforestation
item.description: Some of the world’s top coffee traders and roasters are starting an initiative to map farms around the globe, in a bid to help tackle deforestation and keep the European market open for millions of growers.

item.title: Laps of Icy Roads in China Show Sodium Batteries Making an EV Breakthrough
item.description: Volatile lithium prices and advances in technology mean sodium-ion cells are finally providing a credible alternative for automakers and the energy sector.

item.title: China’s Lead in the Long Duration Energy Storage Race Might Not Last
item.description: The sheer range of technologies for long duration storage could make it hard for China to dominate the supply chain.

item.title: KKR Fund Secures Investment From UAE Blended-Finance Vehicle
item.description: A $30 billion fund backed by the United Arab Emirates has committed to invest in KKR & Co. Inc’s global climate transition fund.

item.title: The US Has a Chance to Rival China in Rush for Longer-Lasting Batteries
item.description: A growing long-duration energy storage sector is an opportunity for competitors to challenge Beijing’s grip on clean technology.

item.title: Czech PM Babis Urges EU to Soften Green Rules to Ease War Impact
item.description: Czech Prime Minister Andrej Babis called on the European Union to relax environmental rules to ease the impact of the energy price spikes caused by the Iran war and to stem a decline in the bloc’s industrial competitiveness.

item.title: Renewables Topped Coal in Global Power Mix in 2025, Ember Says
item.description: Clean power outpaced coal’s share in the world’s electricity supply in 2025 for the first time in more than 100 years and met all the growth in demand, according to a report by global energy think tank Ember.

item.title: UK Plans to Move Wind Farms to New Subsidies to Cut Costs
item.description: The UK will seek to speed up efforts to cut the costs of green electricity by reducing exposure to more-expensive gas.

item.title: Solar Rises to 10% of Japan Power Generation in 2025, Ember Says
item.description: Solar power accounted for 10% of Japan’s electricity generation for the first time last year, exceeding the global average of 9%, according to a report from energy think tank Ember.

item.title: Burgum Cites China Link in Tougher US Reviews for Solar Projects
item.description: US Interior Security Doug Burgum said China’s key role in supplying US solar developments necessitates a more rigorous review process to ferret out any potential threats to national security.

item.title: Trump Invokes Wartime Powers to Fund New Energy Projects
item.description: President Donald Trump invoked the Defense Production Act to provide federal funds for a wide range of energy projects, as his administration faces pressure to help curb rising oil and gas costs.
